possessions.description: The enforcers, informers, thieves and general scum who work for an unseen underworld figure. Who happens to be you.

possessions.name: Honorary Membership in the London Horticultural Society
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Honorary Membership in the London Horticultural Society
possessions.description: Though the Society's renewed Horticultural Show was rudely interrupted by a city-wide catastrophe, the spirit of that heady glasshouse lives on in all who were there to witness the beauty you created.

possessions.name: Membership of God's Editors
possessions.description: Making the good word better.

possessions.name: The London Conspiracy (Newspaper)
possessions.description: Your very own organ of the press. Of course, you will bear this responsibility with grace and integrity.

possessions.name: No One of Consequence
possessions.description: A keepsake from the old masters of Flute Street, and a reminder of forms best not taken.

possessions.name: Strange Aeronaut
possessions.description: Into the depths of dream, and there into the skies beyond. Between the claws of the stars and the knives of the Roof itself.

possessions.name: The Founding Principles of an Infernal Enclave
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x The Founding Principles of an Infernal Enclave
possessions.description: You witnessed the birth of something very new, within something very old.

possessions.name: Your Name in F.F. Gebrandt's Address Book
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Your Name in F.F. Gebrandt's Address Book
possessions.description: She calls on you, sometimes – to pick your brain about the price of emeralds, the movements of the Austrian foreign office, or the fluctuating salinity of the unterzee.

possessions.name: Clay Sedan Chair
possessions.description: The chair is of oak, horsehair and velvet. Only the men carrying it, and you, are clay.

possessions.name: Corpulent Carriage
possessions.description: A misshapen monstrosity, refashioned by the monks of Godfall. Its flank is warm to the touch, its interior plump and comfortable. After long journeys, you fancy you can hear it pant.

possessions.name: Obstreperous Boar
possessions.description: It could almost be a pig – if a pig had skin like a cliff-face, a body the size of a warhorse, and all the benevolent good cheer of a Tyrant's Gardens swan.

possessions.name: Respectable Landau
possessions.description: A steady convertible carriage. Just the sort of thing for visiting maiden aunts and impressionable suitees.

possessions.name: Semi-Automated Mary Lloyd
possessions.description: Is this a child's toy, or a discarded festival invention? The creature's jaw is wired in such a way that as you cycle, its mouth clacks open and closed. Guaranteed to frighten the horses.

possessions.name: Velocipede
possessions.description: "Coming through! Coming through with woefully inefficient brakes!"

possessions.name: At the Shore of Midnight, 5th Edition
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x At the Shore of Midnight, 5th Edition
possessions.description: A nigh-incomprehensible allegorical novel that reads more like the convoluted theogony of a long-forgotten mythology. It concerns a being 'of one body, cataclysmically cleft in twain', and the longing void left behind after its sister-self departs on a vast, dark ocean.

possessions.name: An Elephant That Is Yet to Be Refuted
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x An Elephant That Is Yet to Be Refuted
possessions.description: A fine addition to any room. You can take it anywhere, secure in the knowledge that no one will mention it.

possessions.name: Formidable Basalt Gymnasium
possessions.description: Stone floor and black iron weights. Serious business.

possessions.name: Ichorous Alarm
possessions.description: The blackened blood of one who has strayed too close to the Far Shore of Death. It boils in the presence of mortal peril.

possessions.name: Murgatroyd's Patented Fungal Christmas 'Tree'
possessions.description: Certainly grown in nightsoil. But look, it comes with its own tinsel! And maybe once you're done, you can boil it down for soup.

possessions.name: Portable Lamp-Post
possessions.description: Complete with Farthing & Sons patented telescopic mechanism, it can be transported with ease and style, before being erected at whatever location you consider needs more flattering light.

possessions.name: Unopened Bottle of Broken Giant 1899 'Century Label'
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Unopened Bottle of Broken Giant 1899 'Century Label'
possessions.description: A vintage of sparkling mushroom wine specifically prepared to celebrate the turning of the Century. Given the Empress' decision to have nothing to do with the 20th Century, most such bottles were drunk sadly and alone sometime in the first week of 1899 – the second 1899, that is. Remaining ones have become collector's items, and confer a certain prestige to anyone who held on to them.

possessions.name: Voluminous Library
possessions.description: Like having a thousand erudite friends. Except they don't drink your port and ask to borrow money.

possessions.name: Ogedei-class Liner
possessions.description: Originally designed to glide over the waves and deliver swift hell to the Khagan's enemies. In her new life as a lightly armed passenger vessel, she is even faster.

possessions.name: Celebrated Artist's Model
possessions.description: Talented poet, respected artist, notorious beauty. Cheats at cards.

possessions.name: The Young Stags' Club
possessions.description: What larks we shall have! Fetch the giraffe-spoon, we have a bet to win!

possessions.name: A False-Star of your Own
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A False-Star of your Own
possessions.description: Above London, false-stars shine. One is your bastard child, a Hybrid, a diamond the size of a cow. It is a hundred times brighter than its fellows, a blazing pinpoint; every month or two, for just a few days, it passes directly over the city. For that brief period, London's gloom eases into a velvety twilight.

possessions.name: A Pot of Violant Ink
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A Pot of Violant Ink
possessions.description: Violant is the colour of necessary, but troublesome, connections. The ink tastes like pitch mixed with tears.

possessions.name: Endowment of a University Fellowship
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Endowment of a University Fellowship
possessions.description: One lucky academic now has you to thank for their research position. They have to use your name in all official correspondence. They probably love that. Use to increase your connections at the University up to 20.

possessions.name: Flask of Abominable Salts
possessions.description: Commonplace WARNING: consumption may cause vomiting, paralysis, and ennui.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Academic items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Foxfire Candle Stub
possessions.description: Commonplace Named for its eery greenish glow. It's not made out of foxes.

possessions.name: Memory of Distant Shores
possessions.description: Scarce Your memories of the lands across the Unterzee are something extraordinary. You can spin them into a tale to delight listeners.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Academic items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Formula for Perfumed Gunpowder
possessions.description: Knowing how to make it is worth more than the thing in itself.
possessions.availableAt: You can learn about this item by researching Perfumed Gunpowder at a University Laboratory.

possessions.name: Formula for Railway Steel
possessions.description: How does one make train tracks in quantity?
possessions.availableAt: You can learn this formula by studying Railway Steel at your University Laboratory. You may need to receive a sample from the Dean first.

possessions.name: Incisive Observation
possessions.description: Uncomfortable, but clarifying.
possessions.availableAt: These can be obtained in the Bone Market or occasionally in various locations on the Upper River.

possessions.name: Judgements' Egg
possessions.description: Precious Is this... a soul? It's pale and bright. Souls like this, they say, are the spores of strange gods.
possessions.availableAt: You may be able to find one of these on an expedition to the Tomb of the Silken Thread in the Forgotten Quarter.

possessions.name: Memory of Discordance
possessions.description: Forbidden.

possessions.name: Recipe for Scintillack Snuff
possessions.description: It's one thing to steal the odd consignment. It's another to be able to manufacture it in quantity yourself.

possessions.name: Unprovenanced Artefact
possessions.description: Rare Meticulously crafted by skilled, far-away hands for a deliberate but unknown purpose, and then appropriated, boxed up, and brought to London because "it will look so handsome over the fireplace."
possessions.availableAt: These can be obtained from the Bone Market.

possessions.name: Volume of Collated Research
possessions.description: Commonplace Your collected writings. Or at least, someone's collected writings, with your name on them. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as joining God's Editors.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Academic items in your inventory, or by using a Tale of Terror!! to pursue the story of the Fidgeting Writer.

possessions.name: Confident Smile
possessions.description: Your self-assurance is beguiling. Gives a second chance on Persuasive challenges.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire these by attending to matters of persuasion and scandal at your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Hard-Earned Lesson
possessions.description: You're bruised, but you'll see that trick coming next time.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire these by inviting a friend to tend to your Wounds at your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Hastily Scrawled Warning Note
possessions.description: Last-minute vital information. Gives a second chance on Shadowy challenges.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire these by inviting a friend to join you in something rather shadowy at your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Sudden Insight
possessions.description: Aha! What about -
possessions.availableAt: Acquire these by inviting a friend to help with your Nightmares at your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Fungal Dangers and Poisons: A Guide for New Growers
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Fungal Dangers and Poisons: A Guide for New Growers
possessions.description: This book addresses itself to the novice mushroom farmer, but it omits no detail of the craft. Botanical drawings of two hundred and fifty-two varieties of mushroom. Effects and dosage of toxins. Growing instructions. Lurid anecdotes about Uttershroom exposure. Using this book in combination with Peppercaps will raise your Kataleptic Toxicology if it has not already reached its limit.

possessions.name: Entry in Slowcake's Exceptionals
possessions.description: A line in the only guide to who is reliably whom in Fallen London. Use this to increase your Renown: Society quality by spending Favours: Society. You can also gain Favours: Society with Fate by using the entry. An entry will also allow Persons of Some Importance to raise their Notability on the "A visit from Slowcake's Amanuensis" card, which is essential if you wish to unlock advanced Professions.

possessions.name: O'Boyle's Practical Primer in the Various Languages of Nippon, Tartary, Cathay and the Princedoms of the Raj
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x O'Boyle's Practical Primer in the Various Languages of Nippon, Tartary, Cathay and the Princedoms of the Raj
possessions.description: Claims to give a grounding in most of the major languages of Asia. A bold claim for so slim a book. Use this to increase your Connected: The Widow quality up to 20, and beyond with Fate.

possessions.name: Page from the Liber Visionis
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Page from the Liber Visionis
possessions.description: A page torn from the 'Book of Faces', Benjamin Villein's menacing magnum opus.

possessions.name: Book of Hidden Bodies
possessions.description: Scarce A comprehensive list of who died, who killed them, and where the bodies are buried. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as joining the Parthenaeum.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets. You can also earn one by winning the Tournament of Lilies with your Sinister Sprout.

possessions.name: Copy of your Epic Poetic Cycle
possessions.nameAndLevel: 2 x Copy of your Epic Poetic Cycle
possessions.description: Twelve thousand stanzas of heroism, tragedy and the supernatural.

possessions.name: Copy of your Tale of the Future
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Copy of your Tale of the Future
possessions.description: A bold vision of brass and sparking globes. The story of how London returns to the surface.

possessions.name: Exceptional Short Story
possessions.description: They say this deserves wider recognition.
possessions.availableAt: Create Short Stories at the Activities section of your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Notes on the Case of the Speculative Arachnid
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Notes on the Case of the Speculative Arachnid
possessions.description:
A complex case indeed! The Senior Reader in _______ was blackmailing the Provost of Summerset over secret meetings with a devil. The Senior Reader was also conducting dangerous experiments with the Correspondence, which drove the Radical Postgraduate insane.

The Provost used his trained sorrow-spider to murder the Senior Reader. The vial of Cantigaster venom wasn't relevant to the crime, although it did lead to some startling revelations at the Palace... about the Duchess, the Cantigaster, the Second City, the Bazaar, and about love.

"In the deepest matters of the Bazaar," she said, "look to love. Always".

possessions.name: Page of Cryptopalaeontological Notes
possessions.description: Your research notes on the strange creatures of the Neath. Sort them out at your lodgings when you have enough.
possessions.availableAt: Obtain Cryptopalaeontological Notes on a scientific expedition, then collate them in your lodgings.

possessions.name: Page of Prelapsarian Archaeological Notes
possessions.nameAndLevel: 217 x Page of Prelapsarian Archaeological Notes
possessions.description: Your research notes concerning cities before the Fifth. Sort them out at your lodgings when you have enough.

possessions.name: Page of Theosophistical Notes
possessions.description: Your research notes on matters of the spirit and the other side. Sort them out at your lodgings when you have enough.

possessions.name: Revisionist Historical Narrative
possessions.description: It is selective about its facts and inventive in its interpretations. There are few lies, but a great deal of deception. The work of a Mithridant.
possessions.availableAt: These can be created in the Waswood.

possessions.name: Short Story
possessions.description: They say: a suitable entertainment for a slow evening.
possessions.availableAt: Create Short Stories at the Activities section of your Lodgings.

possessions.name: Slim Volume of Bazaarine Poetry
possessions.nameAndLevel: 3 x Slim Volume of Bazaarine Poetry
possessions.description: Strange metaphors and oblique politics.
possessions.availableAt: This can be obtained by graduating certain students at your Lab.

possessions.name: Unusual Love Story
possessions.description: Affairs of the heart can take peculiar twists down in the Neath.
possessions.availableAt: You can acquire these at Hunter's Keep.

possessions.name: Volume of your Theosophistical Work
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Volume of your Theosophistical Work
possessions.description: You, more than any, are making the world ready for Theosophistry.

possessions.name: Map Scrap
possessions.description: Scarce Enough of these, and you'll know the secrets of earth and sea.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Cartography items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Oneiromantic Revelation
possessions.description: Irem is not so far from Parabola, as falsehood isn't far from future truth. What will be, or what might be, leaks backwards into dreams.

possessions.name: Parabolan Parable
possessions.description: Parabola is a vast mirror, many-fissured. To hope to know the Is-Not is a grand folly – but upon daring dreamers, Parabola occasionally bestows wisdom. This is one such piece, painfully won.
possessions.availableAt: Obtain this through certain campaigns of the War in Parabola, or by combining Oneiromantic Revelations.

possessions.name: Partial Map
possessions.description: Maddeningly incomplete. But perhaps you can make something of it.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Cartography items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Puzzling Map
possessions.description: Scarce Is that right? But that means the path leads to...
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Cartography items in your inventory. Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Shard of Glim
possessions.description: Commonplace The jewels of the Unterzee! Or possibly shards of phosphorescent insect chitin. Authorities differ.

possessions.name: Sighting of a Parabolan Landmark
possessions.nameAndLevel: 794 x Sighting of a Parabolan Landmark
possessions.description: Parabola's towers and trees, its castles and mountains, do not feel any attachment to their positions, and rearrange themselves freely. But for the moment, you have a sighting.

possessions.name: Sky-Story
possessions.description: A tale of an infinite horizon, rich with eternal possibility.

possessions.name: Vitreous Almanac
possessions.description: A far-from-comprehensive guide to Parabola's incomprehensible and boundless terrain. But there is more here than anyone else has yet endeavoured to record.

possessions.name: Zee-Ztory
possessions.description: Scarce Strange truths are told of the waves and what lies beneath. The lies are even stranger.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Cartography items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Collection of Curiosities
possessions.description: Scarce Accoutrements and impedimenta for the elegant life. This can be traded for Puzzling Maps, and may be required for some stories.
possessions.availableAt: You can make these in the Bazaar Side-Streets, and Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Glass Gazette
possessions.description: Crumbling, fragmentary, and inaccurate during nightmares, these pamphlets nonetheless contain the closest thing to a map of Parabola. When one is Lost in Strange Lands, they may be redeemed for Parabolan Scouting.

possessions.name: Screaming Map
possessions.description: This will show you the way to Polythreme. Don't go.

possessions.name: Unusually Loyal Map of London
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Unusually Loyal Map of London
possessions.description: Like all maps in the Neath, it doesn't reliably take you to where you're trying to go. Unlike other maps in the Neath, it sometimes – just sometimes – takes you where you're supposed to be.

possessions.name: Cosmogone-filled Mirrorcatch Box
possessions.description: Filled to the brim by the Parabolan sun. The warmth glows through the smallest crack.
possessions.availableAt: You'll need to start with an empty mirrorcatch box and fill it up, someplace where there's a great deal of cosmogone light.

possessions.name: Comprehensive Bribe
possessions.description: Commonplace This would tempt a judge. Even an honest one. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as buying a Respectable Landau.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets.

possessions.name: Device for the Duplication of Bones
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Device for the Duplication of Bones
possessions.description: Sometimes it goes wrong. Sometimes it forms nodules like grapes but calcium-hard. But its purpose is to copy the bones of a living thing, somewhere outside of its body.

possessions.name: Flawed Diamond
possessions.description: Coveted Why are diamonds so hard to find down here? Does Mr Stones really keep them all for itself?
possessions.availableAt: You can acquire these in various opportunities around the city. If you are a Person of Some Importance you can also buy them at Empire Adornments in the Bazaar Sidestreets.

possessions.name: Hillmover
possessions.description: Do you have a hill? Would you like it to be elsewhere, perhaps in small clumps?
possessions.availableAt: This may be found by doing some hunting in your Parabolan Base-Camp.

possessions.name: London Street Sign
possessions.description: Commonplace These were all supposed to have been destroyed when the Masters made over the city. Don't be caught with them.
possessions.availableAt: Buy these at the Bazaar, or steal them in the Flit.

possessions.name: Magnificent Diamond
possessions.description: Coveted Like a lost star, right there in your palm.
possessions.availableAt: Gain Magnificent Diamonds from certain major crimes, or from the Merry Gentleman with Fate. Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter also occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Mirrorcatch Box
possessions.description: The best way to store light, and certain other mysterious substances. There are few of these, and they are hotly sought-after.
possessions.availableAt: Empty Mirrorcatch Boxes can be obtained at the Fiddler's Scarlet in Jericho Locks, or found, rarely, in the Waswood.

possessions.name: Mr Spices' Private Stash
possessions.description: A quantity of a drug you haven't encountered before. You have only the rumours of where it comes from and what it might be.

possessions.name: Ostentatious Diamond
possessions.description: Coveted Oh, I say.
possessions.availableAt: You can acquire these in various opportunities around the city. If you are a Person of Some Importance you can also buy them at Empire Adornments in the Bazaar Sidestreets. Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Use of Villains
possessions.description: The temporary service of gentlemen with scarred fists, withered scruples and closed mouths. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as forming a Criminal Gang.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets or in the story begun with the Ambitious Barrister informing you 'By the way, someone is trying to kill you'.

possessions.name: Antique Constable's Badge
possessions.description: It's a little dented, a bit tarnished, but this is the badge from a genuine police helmet. Use this to increase your Renown: Constables quality by spending Favours: Constables. You can also gain Favours: Constables with Fate by using the badge.

possessions.name: Deal with a Devil
possessions.description: You've received an invitation to the Brass Embassy. Click on this in your inventory to attend.
possessions.availableAt: Make this by using a Glimpse of Something Larger in your inventory.

possessions.name: Engraved Pewter Tankard
possessions.description: It's got a figurehead for a handle and an engraving of a ship over the word 'Bonny'. It lives behind the bar at the dockside tavern. Use this to increase your Renown: The Docks quality by spending Favours: The Docks. You can also gain Favours: The Docks with Fate by using the tankard.

possessions.name: F.F. Gebrandt's Superior Laudanum
possessions.description: Ensures a deep sleep, but may affect your health. Use when your Nightmares rise too high.

possessions.name: F.F. Gebrandt's Tincture of Vigour
possessions.nameAndLevel: 89 x F.F. Gebrandt's Tincture of Vigour
possessions.description: According to the label, it cures pain, sets bones, curls hair and wards off spiders. Useful if your Wounds quality is rising.

possessions.name: Favourable Circumstance
possessions.description: Someone is smiling on your destiny. Use this at the Send a Message to a Contact storylet in Your Social Engagements to draw certain Opportunity Cards. Note: don't use it if the relevant Card is already in your hand!
possessions.availableAt: You may purchase and use this at the Send a Message to a Contact storylet in Your Social Engagements. It will also refresh every week via 'Time, the healer'.

possessions.name: F.F. Gebrandt's Tincture of Vigour: Half-Full
possessions.nameAndLevel: 3 x F.F. Gebrandt's Tincture of Vigour: Half-Full
possessions.description: According to the label, it cures pain, sets bones, curls hair and wards off spiders. Useful if your Wounds quality is rising.

possessions.name: Implacable Detective's Business Card
possessions.description: Your paths are bound to cross again soon.
possessions.availableAt: Almost any contact with the Implacable Detective will give you one.

possessions.name: Memento of Passion
possessions.description: A souvenir of a familiarity experienced at Court. (Click this to sell at auction - it means you've chosen to forget the affair, and might allow you to explore other options...)

possessions.name: Mysterious Photograph
possessions.description: The subject is blurred, but perhaps you could look closer... Use this item in your inventory.

possessions.name: Nikolas & Sons Instant Ablution Absolution
possessions.nameAndLevel: 5 x Nikolas & Sons Instant Ablution Absolution
possessions.description: Removes the most stubborn, savage & suspicious of stains! Warranted by the Topsy King hisself!

possessions.name: Old Bone Skeleton Key
possessions.description: It's a bit antiquated as lockpicking tools go, but this beautiful bit of ratwork craftsmanship marks you out as a connoisseur of criminality. Use this to increase your Renown: Criminals quality by spending Favours: Criminals. You can also gain Favours: Criminals with Fate by using the key.

possessions.name: Ornate Typewriter
possessions.description: A Fine, Elegant and Robust Feat of Mechanical Engineering! Use this to increase your Renown: Bohemians quality by spending Favours: Bohemians. You can also gain Favours: Bohemians with Fate by using the typewriter.

possessions.name: Professional Perk
possessions.description: Understandings and considerations. Use them in your inventory to gain additional bonuses.
possessions.availableAt: Earn them each week from a Profession, up to a maximum of 4.

possessions.name: Red-Feathered Pin
possessions.description: A tiny, enamelled pin, the secret signal Revolutionaries use to recognise one another. No, it's not decorative. Decorativeness is bourgeois. Use this to increase your Renown: Revolutionaries quality by spending Favours: Revolutionaries. You can also gain Favours: Revolutionaries with Fate by using the pin.

possessions.name: Tiny Jewelled Reliquary
possessions.description: A miniature ivory coffin, containing what is most assuredly the genuine uvula of Saint Leonard of Noblac! Use this to increase your Renown: The Church quality by spending Favours: The Church. You can also gain Favours: The Church with Fate by using the reliquary.

possessions.name: Trade Secret
possessions.description: You've proven yourself in the Professions. You've learnt the lessons that come with time and hard work. You can sell that information on; or perhaps you'll find another use for it.
possessions.availableAt: Practise Professions, and wait...

possessions.name: A Clandestine Rendezvous at Watchmaker's Hill
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A Clandestine Rendezvous at Watchmaker's Hill
possessions.description: "Be at this place, at this time..." This will allow you to choose a Connected Pet if you travel to Watchmaker's Hill.

possessions.name: A Headful of Picaresque Tales
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A Headful of Picaresque Tales
possessions.description: 'IF THERE BE HERE LESSON OR MORAL, IT LIES BEYOND THE COMPETENCE OF HIM WHO INSCRIBES THIS RECORD.' - Emphyrio

possessions.name: A Heart-Shaped Trophy
possessions.description: Seven wins out of at most ten rounds; a remarkable accomplishment.

possessions.name: A Remote Address
possessions.description: You live somewhere out of harm's way.
possessions.availableAt: Moving to certain Lodgings will give you this.

possessions.name: A Scion of a Starved Rose
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A Scion of a Starved Rose
possessions.description: Pale, flexile, and aromatic. Keep it in a vase of amber, and prepare to be surprised.

possessions.name: An Invitation to dinner at the Temple Club
possessions.nameAndLevel: 3 x An Invitation to dinner at the Temple Club
possessions.description: Allows entrance to the club hidden in the ruins of a temple in the Forgotten Quarter. Valid for one visit only.

possessions.name: Betrayer of Measures
possessions.description: It is a ruler that does not rule and a scale that does not weigh, a device inscribed with Correspondence and a product of the Red Science. When it works, it can make small things much larger, and large things much smaller. When it does not work – which is most of the time – it brings about excruciating distortions and gives researchers a swelled head.

possessions.name: Black Ribbon
possessions.description: A scrap of black ribbon to be worn on the arm. This denotes that you have permanently killed a member of the Black Ribbon duelling society.

possessions.name: Broken Toy
possessions.description: What could be more charming than this painted tin reminder of childhood innocence?

possessions.name: Carnival Ticket
possessions.description: 'Allowing entrance to the WONDERS and MARVELS of Mrs Plenty's MOST DISTRACTING CARNIVAL.'

possessions.name: Coilheart Games 1899 Commemorative Archway
possessions.description: Real wreckage from the Labyrinth's fallen amphitheatres, scavenged and repurposed into a dazzling collector's item! Requires structural reinforcement.

possessions.name: Counterfeit Constellation
possessions.description: The false-stars have shifted to herald a new child.

possessions.name: Daguerreotype with Fading Music-Hall Singer's Address
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Daguerreotype with Fading Music-Hall Singer's Address
possessions.description: It's at the bad end of Blythenhale. She really was on the way down.

possessions.name: Dirty Secret
possessions.description: They would not like you knowing this. Oh no, they would not like that at all.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire this by performing Election Flash Lays and exchanging Cellars of Wine and Criminal Favours on the Candidate Resources card.

possessions.name: Distinction of Hearts, Bronze
possessions.description:
'OUR HYPOTHESIS ENCOMPASSES THREE FORMS. The first form is quick as Law, bright and brutal. To this form belongs the Hour of the Hunt, the arts of dawn and ambush, and the savageries of Knife & Candle. Its fruit is violence, its taste sudden and final. It is unsuitable for our purpose.'

A memento of Hearts' Game. Awarded to all who win at least one round in a given season.

possessions.name: Distinction of Hearts, Gold
possessions.description:
'THE THIRD FORM is that-which-never-comes, sought but never seen. To this form belongs the infinite. It owns the approach and never the arrival. Its fruit is life, hollowed with the hearts-blood of the father of asps. The conjecture is thus: the hour of our funeral may be pushed beyond the curve of eternity.' (This one is annotated: Perpetuity Postulates, I. iv.)

A memento of Hearts' Game. Awarded to those who earn a trophy over the course of a given season.

possessions.name: Distinction of Hearts, Silver
possessions.description:
'THE SECOND FORM is slow; burgeoning; botanical. It strangles like ivy, blooming like rot in the hollows of the chest. To this form belongs poison, time, and ennui, clutched in the crook of a wing like a lover. Its fruit lingers like blood on the tongue, and with every bite the taste persists yet longer. We stretch this form's limits as far as we may.'

A memento of Hearts' Game. Awarded to those who win at least seven rounds in a season.

possessions.name: Fillip of Effervescence
possessions.description: Possesses a giddy headiness. Has a sharp flavour on the tongue, but a long, warm finish. Blends well with port, and strong coffee.

possessions.name: Fragment of a Black Coral Sculpture
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Fragment of a Black Coral Sculpture
possessions.description: At least you think it's part of a sculpture. It could be anything, really.

possessions.name: Free Evening
possessions.description: Cherish them. (And you thought diamonds were rare.) They are essential for certain social actions.
possessions.availableAt: Time, the Healer permits five each week.

possessions.name: Ginger-Haired Fellow's Address
possessions.description: Elderwick. Behind a church, apparently.

possessions.name: Horsehead Amulet
possessions.description: Clutch this to your breast and it brings you health, they say. And a reputation for gullibility. This will save you, once, when you die in the Fifth City.

possessions.name: Leathery Human Heart
possessions.description: Peculiar and unpleasant. The heart has been branded with the word WOLFSTACK. Like the Docks?

possessions.name: Locket Depicting Twin Sisters
possessions.description: It has portraits of the Fading Music-Hall Singer and her sister.

possessions.name: Mostly-Faithful Ushabti Replica (1:4 Scale)
possessions.description: Expertly assembled, but clearly not the real thing. An inexpensive reminder of what Londoners endured. And a beautiful piece of Egyptian art to display on your mantelpiece – just hide it if any Masters come round for tea.

possessions.name: Myrrh-Scented Rose
possessions.description: Fresh roses from the Surface that have been lightly embalmed for longevity. They still wither away - just more slowly.

possessions.name: Painting Fragment
possessions.description: One of the toffs that comes down to Veilgarden might recognise it.

possessions.name: Public Attention
possessions.description: They will hear what you have to say.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire this by attending Parties, conduction Election Flash Lays, publishing a special Election edition of your newspaper, and exchanging Docks Favours on the Candidate Resources card.

possessions.name: Rose-Ringed Regimental Helmet
possessions.description: Scorched, packed with thorns, and still bearing Southwark's regimental insignia. A reminder of wars past and future.

possessions.name: Sapphire
possessions.description: They import these things from the Carnelian Coast. But they're sapphires. Clear? Excellent.
possessions.availableAt: You can win these with an outstanding performance when lizard-throwing at Mrs. Plenty's Carnival. If you are a Person of Some Importance you can also buy them at Empire Adornments in the Bazaar Sidestreets.

possessions.name: Set of Correspondence Stones
possessions.description: A set of seven carved stones from a king's tomb in the Forgotten Quarter. Turn them over in your hand, and the glyphs brim with light as if water pours through them.

possessions.name: Stone Tentacle-Key
possessions.description: What's this carved from? Flowstone? Dark bone? Grey amber?
possessions.availableAt: Gain this at Hunter's Keep

possessions.name: The Book of Stolen Endings
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x The Book of Stolen Endings
possessions.description: another way things might have been

possessions.name: The Demon, Tattered
possessions.description: A Hallowmas Mask depicting a Christian demon, too tattered from the intensity of the revels to be worn.

possessions.name: The Moth, Wingless
possessions.description: A Hallowmas mask of a moth. The wings are barely hanging on by a thread; it's much too fragile to wear.

possessions.name: Venom-Ruby
possessions.description: A single drop of the Cantigaster's blood. Scintillating. Lethal. Don't put it in your mouth.
possessions.availableAt: You can win these at the Shroom-Hopping Track. If you are a Person of Some Importance you can also buy them at Empire Adornments in the Bazaar Sidestreets.

possessions.name: Vindication of Faith
possessions.description: A first confrontation with Hell can bring relief, even joy.

possessions.name: First City Coin
possessions.description: One side bears what might be a cedar tree. You've never met anyone who can read the script on the other side.

possessions.name: Amber Ha'penny
possessions.description: Tiny, sticky, and stamped with the image of a breaking chain.

possessions.name: Assortment of Khaganian Coinage
possessions.description: Their value is standardized, but each clan mints its own: squarish coins with the face of a leopard; octagonal tokens with encircling taimen; large banknotes branded with a horse's head.

possessions.name: Estival Tokens
possessions.description: The currency of summer. Use this to purchase items at the Marquee of Mystification during the yearly July event.

possessions.name: Fistful of Surface Currency
possessions.description: Commonplace Pounds, francs, dollars, piastres... for spies, collectors and patriots.
possessions.availableAt: Steal this from certain drunks in Spite, or call in favours in the Shuttered Palace.

possessions.name: Forged Justificande Coin
possessions.description: It looks much like a real Justificande, but it doesn't have the same effect on nearby clocks.

possessions.name: Fourth-City Echo
possessions.description: Hudum writing on the reverse; on the obverse, the familiar profile of a spire. Unmistakeably the Bazaar's currency.

possessions.name: Hinterland Scrip
possessions.description: Issued by the Ministry of Upper River Development. Not a Ministry one has heard of until very recently. It represents the right to develop new buildings on a portion of the land.
possessions.availableAt: Hinterland Scrip may be obtained in the Bone Market, in other opportunities, and by selling items in the Upper River.

possessions.name: Justificande Coin
possessions.description: ONE DAY YOU WILL FORGIVE is stamped on the back. It is a commandment as much as a promise.
possessions.availableAt: This may be stolen, if you know where to look.

possessions.name: Mark of Credit Page
possessions.description: A Mark of Credit is a promise of a treasure from the Bazaar. You have here a page from one.

possessions.name: Penny
possessions.description: One hundred pence makes an Echo. One hundred Echoes will purchase you a marvel or two at the Bazaar.
possessions.availableAt: Sell almost anything at the Bazaar.

possessions.name: Antique Mystery
possessions.description: Commonplace A secret from before the Fall. Before the First City? Before the Bazaar?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Elder items in your inventory, or by using a Tale of Terror!! to pursue the story of the Fidgeting Writer. With a high shadowy you can also steal them in high-profile burglaries from the Flit.

possessions.name: Jade Fragment
possessions.description: Commonplace So shiny! It's fossilised souls, you know.
possessions.availableAt: You can buy this from the Bazaar, or use the Implacable Detective's Business Card.

possessions.name: Mystery of the Elder Continent
possessions.nameAndLevel: 35 x Mystery of the Elder Continent
possessions.description: Scarce An intriguing scrap of knowledge about a place far across the Unterzee. What can it mean?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Elder items in your inventory. Opportunities to acquire these can be found by increasing your connections to the Rubbery Men, Revolutionaries, Urchins and Hell.

possessions.name: Presbyterate Passphrase
possessions.description: Scarce A formula that can be used, once, for a peculiar purpose in the distant Presbyterate.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Elder items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Primaeval Hint
possessions.description: Coveted A distant echo: an apprehension of something impossibly ancient. What will the Masters pay for this?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Elder items in your inventory. Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Relic of the Third City
possessions.nameAndLevel: 87 x Relic of the Third City
possessions.description: Commonplace Cinnabar beads and little square granite gods.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Elder items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Attar
possessions.levelDescription: You have Attar in your eyes
possessions.description: The commodity of Arbor; a reward for wonder and novelty; a thick dust that gathers behind the eyelashes.

possessions.name: Certifiable Scrap
possessions.description: Perhaps Mr Cups will recognise this as something delightful. When the Relickers appear, you'll be able to trade these Scraps for many things...
possessions.availableAt: Get these from lodging cards and other opportunities around London.

possessions.name: Crate of Expedition Supplies
possessions.description: Tools, assistants, victuals, knowledge, fresh water, messenger-bats... it takes time and effort to bring these to the Forgotten Quarter.
possessions.availableAt: Purchase these before you begin an Expedition. If you run out, the Expedition will end.

possessions.name: Knob of Scintillack
possessions.description: Coveted Coral that glimmers as if moonlit. It makes marvellous jewellery or terribly expensive tea.

possessions.name: Piece of Rostygold
possessions.description: Traditionally, you're paid in this if you kill something. Note: may not contain actual gold.
possessions.availableAt: Many tasks in Watchmaker's Hill pay out in rostygold.

possessions.name: Preparations for a Trip through the Mirror
possessions.nameAndLevel: 9 x Preparations for a Trip through the Mirror
possessions.description: True preparedness is impossible; what you have is the illusion of readiness.

possessions.name: Preserved Surface Blooms
possessions.description: Safe in an airless bubble, scented beyond price.
possessions.availableAt: This can be obtained at the Bone Market or through smuggling in the Upper River.

possessions.name: Copper Cipher Ring
possessions.description: Intricate little interlocking wheels let you code and decode messages. And make delightfully satisfying little clicking sounds. Use this to increase your Renown: The Great Game quality by spending Favours: The Great Game. You can also gain Favours: The Great Game with Fate by using the ring.

possessions.name: Moves in the Great Game
possessions.nameAndLevel: 7 x Moves in the Great Game
possessions.description: Commonplace The nations of Europe, and beyond, compete for influence. Their plots reach even to the Neath.
possessions.availableAt: Obtain this from the Bone Market or in Parabola.

possessions.name: Stalemate
possessions.description: Neither side wins.
possessions.availableAt: Gain a stalemate at the Chessboard in Parabola.

possessions.name: Vital Intelligence
possessions.description: Rare Vitality and intelligence. What would we do without them? Especially if we're spies, diplomats, or pirates.
possessions.availableAt: These can be obtained from heists at the Flit, or in the Bone Market through a certain furtive exchange.

possessions.name: Well-Placed Pawn
possessions.description: The piece is nothing. The position is all.

possessions.name: Nicatorean Relic
possessions.description: Either this is evidence of an ancient king in the Presbyterate, or it is d__ing evidence of Mithridatic fabrication of an ancient king in the Presbyterate.
possessions.availableAt: Expeditions into the Moulin waste sometimes uncover these.

possessions.name: Relic of the Fourth City
possessions.nameAndLevel: 681 x Relic of the Fourth City
possessions.description: Horsehead amulets carved from bone and blue-glazed potsherds.
possessions.availableAt: These are often found in the Forbidden Quarter.

possessions.name: Relic of the Second City
possessions.nameAndLevel: 207 x Relic of the Second City
possessions.description: Gypsum heads and indecipherable clay tablets.

possessions.name: Trace of the First City
possessions.nameAndLevel: 25 x Trace of the First City
possessions.description: The print of a sandal in the mud, vitrified into permanence. Someone walked this way, long ago.

possessions.name: Amanita Sherry
possessions.description: Scarce You might drink this if you were a devil. Or if you hadn't learnt to read labels.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Infernal items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Bright Brass Skull
possessions.description: Commonplace A dear little trinket of the sort favoured by devils. Use this to increase your Renown: Hell quality by spending Favours: Hell. You can also gain Favours: Hell with Fate by using the skull.
possessions.availableAt: Trade Certifiable Scraps for this with the Capering Relicker, or buy it at the Bazaar.

possessions.name: Brilliant Soul
possessions.description: Commonplace This was the soul of someone exceptional. A priest? A poet? A murderer of distinction?
possessions.availableAt: Create this using other Infernal items in your inventory, or steal them in the Flit.

possessions.name: Muscaria Brandy
possessions.description: Commonplace Mortals don't drink this reeking oily fluid, at least not twice. Devils prize it, possibly for its effect on mortals.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Infernal items in your inventory. With a high Shadowy you can also steal them in high-profile burglaries from the Flit.

possessions.name: Soul
possessions.description: Commonplace It's not strictly legal to trade these without a licence, you know. But it's profitable.

possessions.name: Devilbone Die
possessions.description: Does Hell load its dice? With what? Gunpowder?
possessions.availableAt: These are the very devil to find.

possessions.name: Infernal Machine
possessions.description: More than a contraption. More than a device. This is the handmaiden of industry. It clanks and steams; it cuts material and bends metal; it runs very hot.

possessions.name: Queer Soul
possessions.description: Rare A remarkable, almost unclassifiable specimen with very particular qualities. A connoisseur-devil might pay a premium for this.

possessions.name: Silent Soul
possessions.description: Rare Most souls bob and peer. This one simply watches. Serene? Empty? Old, certainly.
possessions.availableAt: These are possible to acquire through a particular heist.

possessions.name: Compromising Document
possessions.description: Commonplace Who was so unwise as to leave this lying around? Perhaps you'd better look after it.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Influence items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Intriguing Snippet
possessions.description: Scarce ...really? With who? This is worth remembering...
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Influence items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Stolen Correspondence
possessions.description: Commonplace Good heavens. People do these things? And then write them down?
possessions.availableAt: Acquire this in the Flit, Empress' Court or University, or buy it at the Bazaar.

possessions.name: Stolen Kiss
possessions.description: Commonplace Something to treasure – or boast of.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Influence items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Favour in High Places
possessions.description: Scarce Perhaps the only currency that is truly worth accumulating. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as acquiring a Majestic Pleasure Yacht.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Influence items in your inventory. If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can also create it in the Bazaar Sidestreets or use the Polite Invitation in your lodgings. Campaigners will receive them periodically.

possessions.name: Personal Recommendation
possessions.description: Scarce A bona fide from an individual of impeccable reputation. It might even be real. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as joining The Parthenaeum.
possessions.availableAt: You can earn this by winning the Tournament of Lilies with your Sinister Sprout. Or, if you are a Person of Some Importance, you can receive it from the Polite Invitation opportunity card, or may create it in the Bazaar Sidestreets

possessions.name: Secluded Address
possessions.description: Coveted The labyrinths of London have a thousand hidden corners. You have uncovered one of value.

possessions.name: Blood Oath
possessions.description: A red promise, commemorated by a scar.

possessions.name: Cave-Aged Code of Honour
possessions.description: Persisting millennia in the dark where no laws survive.
possessions.availableAt: This may be found in the deepest levels of the Magistracy of the Evenlode, if you know what to look for.

possessions.name: Dubious Testimony
possessions.description: Coveted I saw it! Ask anyone! ...except her. Don't ask her.
possessions.availableAt: Surprise Packages sometimes contain this. It is a possible reward for arson. Watchers and Journalists also receive it periodically.

possessions.name: Fragment of the Tragedy Procedures
possessions.nameAndLevel: 3 x Fragment of the Tragedy Procedures
possessions.description: Precious Take comfort – there are arrangements for every reckoning.
possessions.availableAt: These can be stolen through a special heist in the Flit – if you can find the location where they are kept.

possessions.name: Infernal Contract
possessions.description: Commonplace Long since expired, but lawyers love these things.
possessions.availableAt: Buy these from the Bazaar or steal them in the Flit.

possessions.name: Legal Document
possessions.description: Commonplace 'The party of the first part will part with the part of the part...' This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as pursuing a case for a Voluminous Library.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets.

possessions.name: Seed of the Cedar
possessions.description: The Cedar is a guarantor of oaths and promises. An order between persons, not made of laws. It looks like a small dark-brown kernel. It means a great deal more.

possessions.name: Sworn Statement
possessions.description: Commonplace It may have been true before. It's certainly true now.
possessions.availableAt: Available through the Watcher and Journalist Professions.

possessions.name: Key to a Cottage by the Observatory
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Key to a Cottage by the Observatory
possessions.description: The blind men at the Observatory sell these, to people who don't mind occasionally being assaulted by predatory fungus in the middle of the night. Use the key in your Inventory to move into your new lodgings. Allows three cards in your hand.

possessions.name: Key to a Dripstone-Snared Temple
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Key to a Dripstone-Snared Temple
possessions.description: A notched dolomite rod. Using this will change your current lodgings to your Dripstone-Snared Temple. Allows three cards in your hand.

possessions.name: Key to a Handsome Townhouse
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Key to a Handsome Townhouse
possessions.description: Let's see - graceful, well-appointed, with a respectable address and a number of wide, airy rooms. 'Airy' if they weren't a mile underground, anyway. Use the key in your Inventory to move into your new lodgings. Allows three cards in your hand.

possessions.name: Key to a Zee-Znail Retreat
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Key to a Zee-Znail Retreat
possessions.description: An ordinary-looking little key for an extraordinary dwelling. Using this will change your current lodgings to your Zee-Znail Retreat. Allows three cards in your hand.

possessions.name: Lump of Lamplighter Beeswax
possessions.description: Commonplace The poisons of the lamplighter bee make collecting its wax a dangerous job. The price, however, is right.

possessions.name: Memory of Light
possessions.description: You saw something inexplicable. A rotting, succulent light lingers in your memories... This item can be sold... but the merchants of the Bazaar are not permitted to offer cash for it, only secrets.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Luminosity items in your inventory, or via work with the Implacable Detective.

possessions.name: Mourning Candle
possessions.description: Commonplace They give out a smudgy, dolorous light. From the tomb-colonies, but it's best not to ask how they're made.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Luminosity items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Phosphorescent Scarab
possessions.description: Scarce Cheap but lamentably mobile substitute for candles. Evidence of Londoners' inordinate fondness for beetles.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Luminosity items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Deconstructive Lens
possessions.description: A warp in the light; a twist in the real. A crystal that poisons the view.

possessions.name: Mountain-sherd
possessions.description: Precious This was struck from the side of the Mountain-of-Light, who is the god called Stone. It is holy: treat it accordingly.

possessions.name: Ray-Drenched Cinder
possessions.description: Rare Sizzling with violant and irrigo light. It has absorbed radiations not of this earth. Some of its colours can only be seen in sleep.

possessions.name: Appalling Secret
possessions.description: Commonplace Are you quite sure you want to know this?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Mysteries items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Cryptic Clue
possessions.description: Commonplace Information too dangerous to be written down, except in code.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Mysteries items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Extraordinary Implication
possessions.description: Commonplace How can such a thing be? Who but you could understand what it means?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Mysteries items in your inventory, or by using a Tale of Terror!! to pursue the story of the Fidgeting Writer.

possessions.name: Journal of Infamy
possessions.description: Commonplace Here is recorded wickedness to freeze the blood and ravage the soul!
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Mysteries items in your inventory, or via work with the Implacable Detective. With a high shadowy you can also steal them in high-profile burglaries from the Flit.

possessions.name: Tale of Terror!!
possessions.description: Commonplace The blood freezes! The neck-hairs rise! The spine crackles with fear! The audience is spellbound!
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Mysteries items in your inventory. With a high Shadowy you can also steal them in high-profile burglaries from the Flit.

possessions.name: Whispered Hint
possessions.description: Commonplace Inscribed in tiny letters on a slip of paper small enough to tie to a raven's leg.

possessions.name: Caustic Apocryphon
possessions.description: Of dubious provenance and unsettling contents. An accounting of events inimical to history as you know it.

possessions.name: Direful Reflection
possessions.description: Coveted An idea has taken root. Perhaps it's valuable, but consider not too closely, lest ye be considered.
possessions.availableAt: Acquire this by playing the Great Game in Veilgarden, or by meeting with the Interpreter of Dreams.

possessions.name: Searing Enigma
possessions.description: Coveted Mysteries are fire. Truth burns.
possessions.availableAt: Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them. Shadowy Dealings at the University can manufacture them, at considerable expense.

possessions.name: Uncanny Incunabulum
possessions.description: Scarce A venerable book on the very brink of tome-hood, pages crackling with mystery.
possessions.availableAt: Create this using other Mysteries in your inventory.

possessions.name: Drop of Prisoner's Honey
possessions.description: Commonplace The most delightful secret of the Neath: the honey of lamplighter bees fed exclusively on the Exile's Rose.

possessions.name: Emetic Revelation
possessions.description: Some truths are too poisonous to stay buried.

possessions.name: Romantic Notion
possessions.description: Scarce When buying these, the Bazaar borders on generosity. Do the Masters aspire to poetry?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Nostalgia items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Touching Love Story
possessions.description: Scarce Only the flintiest heart could claim to be unmoved. And they sell well at the Bazaar.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Nostalgia items in your inventory or steal them in the Flit.

possessions.name: Vision of the Surface
possessions.description: Scarce Wistful souls in London will pay dearly for news of the sky.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Nostalgia items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Bazaar Permit
possessions.description: Commonplace Peculiar and sinister privileges committed to gently rippling paper. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as acquiring a ship.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Nostalgia items in your inventory. Or, if you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create it in the Bazaar Sidestreets. With a high Shadowy you can also steal them in high-profile burglaries from the Flit.

possessions.name: Captivating Ballad
possessions.description: Coveted Grief enough to drown an ocean. Love enough to ruin a city.

possessions.name: Soothe & Cooper Long-Box
possessions.description: Rare Coffins are uncommon in London. This one is stencilled and franked - a coffin with purpose.

possessions.name: Vestige of a Starlit Reverie
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Vestige of a Starlit Reverie
possessions.description: For a moment, there, you thought you had wings.

possessions.name: Bone Fragments
possessions.description: The residue of Palaeontological work.

possessions.name: Femur of a Jurassic Beast
possessions.nameAndLevel: 38 x Femur of a Jurassic Beast
possessions.description: It comes from the Surface, but the Surface a very long time ago.
possessions.availableAt: These can be achieved by studying an Unidentified Thigh Bone at your Laboratory.

possessions.name: Femur of a Surface Deer
possessions.nameAndLevel: 10 x Femur of a Surface Deer
possessions.description: Still smells faintly of venison.
possessions.availableAt: These can sometimes be found when excavating in Ealing Gardens or elsewhere, when a search for more lucrative bones fails.

possessions.name: Fin Bones, Collected
possessions.description: A fin has as many bones as a hand or a foot. It's a bother finding all of them.

possessions.name: Headless Skeleton
possessions.description: It's a skeleton – rib cage, arms, legs, the lot – except for the skull. The skull's missing.

possessions.name: Helical Thighbone
possessions.description: Curiously twisted. The foot would have pointed off to one side.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found by excavation in Ealing Gardens, or in Flute Street.

possessions.name: Holy Relic of the Thigh of Saint Fiacre
possessions.nameAndLevel: 9 x Holy Relic of the Thigh of Saint Fiacre
possessions.description: There are marks at the hip joint where it used to be gilded and studded with diamonds.
possessions.availableAt: These can be acquired by studying an Unidentified Thigh Bone in your Laboratory and then choosing not to be wholly honest about the identification.

possessions.name: Horned Skull
possessions.description: The horns would supply a very creditable Viking.
possessions.availableAt: The Butcher in Ealing Gardens can create one of these from an aurochs, or you may find one by chance at Bullbone Island.

possessions.name: Human Arm
possessions.description: What's a little grave robbery among friends?

possessions.name: Human Ribcage
possessions.description: Not any friend of yours.
possessions.availableAt: You can buy these at the Bone Market for an unreasonable amount, or call in Constable Favours to receive them at the Shuttered Palace.

possessions.name: Jet Black Stinger
possessions.description: It looks like the back half of a primordial scorpion, long since converted to a menacing fossil. The stone is black and smooth as a mirror.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found via opportunities in Winewound Heath or by going on protective expeditions to Aescwine Hill in Ealing Gardens.

possessions.name: Knotted Humerus
possessions.description: It's a figure-of-eight knot, executed in bone.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found from an opportunity card in London, or by excavation in Ealing Gardens.

possessions.name: Leviathan Frame
possessions.description: Something that swam deep and swallowed the unwary.

possessions.name: Mammoth Ribcage
possessions.description: The original animal must have been roughly the size of a chapel.
possessions.availableAt: Given the right technology, you can create these in your Laboratory from human ribcages.

possessions.name: Plaster Tail Bones
possessions.description: They're a reproduction of what the bones in a tail might look like.

possessions.name: Preserved False-Snake
possessions.description: The body is preserved in a jar: legless, scaleless, deep blue. There are tiny tentacles on its face, below its sightless eyes. This item can usefully be studied in a laboratory, if you have one.

possessions.name: Sabre-toothed Skull
possessions.description: The prey of this creature must have been armoured. It's the only explanation for so much puncturing power.
possessions.availableAt: The skull can be acquired from a Somnolent Hyena, given a cooperative butcher.

possessions.name: Survey of the Neath's Bones
possessions.nameAndLevel: 242 x Survey of the Neath's Bones
possessions.description: Geology, history, layers of rock.

possessions.name: Thorned Ribcage
possessions.description: Not human. It might belong to an ex-goat, or a very small horse, or a dog of some kind. Unexplained bone spurs adhere to the lower three ribs on each side, and all along the spine.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found by performing excavations in Ealing Gardens, or as a rare effect in Parabola.

possessions.name: Unidentified Thigh Bone
possessions.description: Who knows what it came from?
possessions.availableAt: These may be received from the Dean at the University Lab, begged for at the Bone Market, or acquired through assorted other searches.

possessions.name: Withered Tentacle
possessions.description: This thing is dried flesh rather than fossilised bone. But it does suggest a shape, does it not?

possessions.name: Silk Scrap
possessions.description: Commonplace There probably can't be that many spiders down here. Probably.

possessions.name: Surface-Silk Scrap
possessions.description: Commonplace None of your sorrow-spider squeezings. This came out of a real Surface worm.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rag Trade items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Thirsty Bombazine Scrap
possessions.description: This weave absorbs light and water equally, and grows heavy with the light it eats. For the richest of mourners... or the most demanding of thieves.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rag Trade items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Whisper-Satin Scrap
possessions.description: Scarce Woven in Polythreme, they say, from the sweetest of voices.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rag Trade items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Puzzle-Damask Scrap
possessions.description: Scarce Elaborately woven patterns in the richest colours. Each is a secret to be unravelled with the cloth.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rag Trade items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Baptised Rattus Faber Corpse
possessions.description: Rare The bounty on these things is substantial. Don't be deceived by its serene repose. Look at that glazed, sinister ratty eye! Look at the wicked edge on that tiny knife!

possessions.name: Rat of Glory
possessions.description: Precious The rare and coveted Rat of Glory! Fabricated from an L.B. master-thief, wick'd with puzzle-damask, tinted with the inks of the undernight. The Scuttering Company has been known to place its cunningest agents into a death-like sleep and disguise them as Rats of Glory, to smuggle them into candle-collections. This item may come awake at any time, in which case any candles or Luminosity items you possess are at risk. You may eliminate this risk by ridding yourself of it.

possessions.name: Rat on a String
possessions.description: Commonplace A sad little furry body ready to be turned in for the reward, or boiled as the starring ingredient in a tasty stew.

possessions.name: Ratty Reliquary
possessions.description: Rare To what standard must a rat live to be remembered in gold and jewels? Must their superiority be moral? Spiritual? Artisanal?

possessions.name: Venge-Rat Corpse
possessions.description: Coveted The L.B.s, the most cunning of ratkind, train venge-rats as instruments of their will. The well-disciplined venge-rat is a sneak-thief, an assassin, deadly intent on the ravagement of pantry and parlour!
possessions.availableAt: This can be stolen from Rat-Catchers in the Pickpocket's Promenade.

possessions.name: Nodule of Pulsating Amber
possessions.description: Commonplace It looks like a reddish chunk of Deep Amber, but if you hold it in your hand, it beats like a little heart. Use this to increase your Renown: Rubbery Men quality by spending Favours: Rubbery Men. You can also gain Favours: Rubbery Men with Fate by using the amber.

possessions.name: Nodule of Deep Amber
possessions.description: Commonplace Warmly glowing and, er, slightly warm.

possessions.name: Nodule of Trembling Amber
possessions.description: Coveted It thrills with electric life. Something in it glows like the very most distant of suns.
possessions.availableAt: Trembling Amber can be obtained from certain unorthodox transactions with Rubbery Men, and occasionally from Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter.

possessions.name: Unearthly Fossil
possessions.description: Coveted Some things that are a little like bones, trapped in something a little like green stone. Not, as they say, from around these parts.

possessions.name: An Identity Uncovered!
possessions.description: Scarce Good Lord! That was them? All along?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rumour items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Diary of the Dead
possessions.description: Commonplace Heavily foxed, crumbling parchment, bound in suitably unpleasant flaking leather. It certainly looks like a diary of the dead. Use this to increase your Renown: Tomb-Colonies quality by spending Favours: Tomb-Colonies. You can also gain Favours: Tomb-Colonies with Fate by using the diary.
possessions.availableAt: Trade Certifiable Scraps for this with the Curt Relicker, or buy it at the Bazaar.

possessions.name: Inkling of Identity
possessions.description: Scarce Have you glimpsed the face behind the mask?
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rumour items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Proscribed Material
possessions.description: Commonplace The censors are empowered to confiscate this stuff. But why should they have all the fun?

possessions.name: Scrap of Incendiary Gossip
possessions.description: Scarce Unthinkable! Unrepeatable! Inescapable!
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rumour items in your inventory, or by using the Implacable Detective's Business Card.

possessions.name: Blackmail Material
possessions.description: Commonplace Step carefully. This knowledge is dangerous to both you and them.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Rumour items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Night on the Town
possessions.description: Scarce The necessary arrangements for a box at the opera and an elegant supper for two. Or a boozy knees-up for forty. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as charming the Artist's Model.
possessions.availableAt: Create these in the Bazaar Side-streets.  Journalists also receive them periodically.

possessions.name: Rumour of the Upper River
possessions.nameAndLevel: 22 x Rumour of the Upper River
possessions.description: There are all sorts of tales about life further up the Stolen River. Most of them have to be false.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found throughout the Upper River.

possessions.name: Dark-Dewed Cherry
possessions.description: Fresh fruit! Down here? On which strange tree did these little beauties grow?

possessions.name: Darkdrop Coffee
possessions.description: Musky! Explosive! Almost certainly not actually brewed from bat guano! That's just Mr Wines' little joke.

possessions.name: Magnificent Feast
possessions.description: The chops may not be exactly lamb, and the vegetables may not bear close investigation by the squeamish. Nevertheless: rich and nourishing! This will have a wonderful effect on your Wounds, your Nightmares, and your Irrigo, as well as other beneficial consequences.

possessions.name: Tin of Murgatroyd's Jellied Mushroom Cakes
possessions.nameAndLevel: 15 x Tin of Murgatroyd's Jellied Mushroom Cakes
possessions.description: More of a biscuit, really. And more of a mould than mushroom. Still, after eating one, you are more attuned to the world around you and its opportunities. Especially those opportunities that in no way involve jellied mushroom.

possessions.name: A Recipe for Zzoup
possessions.description: Winewound chanterelles, exile's rose, paprika, pork stock, lament-onions, hanged men's tears, salt.
possessions.availableAt: Who knowzz?

possessions.name: Basket of Rubbery Pies
possessions.description: Rubbery lumps, encased in a rubbery crust.
possessions.availableAt: These can be obtained at the Bone Market.

possessions.name: Beatrice's Taper-nut
possessions.description: A dark brown, elongated nut, almost the shape of a candle. It gives a soft gold light when burned, or a delicious nut-paste if roasted and pounded down.

possessions.name: Crate of Incorruptible Biscuits
possessions.description: They never go off. Arguably they were never on.
possessions.availableAt: These can be obtained at the Bone Market.

possessions.name: Hand-picked Peppercaps
possessions.description: The Upper River farmlands grow these abundantly and sell them by the crate. You never know how concentrated the spice will be until you bite down on one.
possessions.availableAt: These can be found in various places around Ealing and the Upper River.

possessions.name: Jasmine Leaves
possessions.description: A jealously-guarded blend. Some say it comes from the Widow's own warehouses, others that it was picked in the Elder Continent.

possessions.name: Magisterial Lager
possessions.description: "Brewed from the waters of the Evenlode, under the nose of the Law." A grinning judge graces the label. His Honour must not have a sense of smell.
possessions.availableAt: Purchase this from the Upper River markets.

possessions.name: Pot of Venison Marrow
possessions.description: It isn't butter, but there are some that prefer it.
possessions.availableAt: The butcher at Ealing Gardens can make this for you.

possessions.name: Remains of a Pinewood Shark
possessions.nameAndLevel: 3 x Remains of a Pinewood Shark
possessions.description: It can be rendered as steaks, or soup, or a preparation of jellied fins. If it would stop staring at you.
possessions.availableAt: This can be found through an advanced hunt in Parabola.

possessions.name: Sausage About Which No One Complains
possessions.nameAndLevel: 4 x Sausage About Which No One Complains
possessions.description: You'll never hear a word spoken against it.
possessions.availableAt: The butcher at Ealing Gardens can make this for you, if you have Mutersalt.

possessions.name: Solacefruit
possessions.description: Commonplace In smaller quantities, delightful and refreshing. In larger quantities, one of the very nicest ways to die.

possessions.name: Apostate's Psalm
possessions.description: A fragment of a forsaken liturgy, stricken from the canon and altered by exile. A whisper of a forgotten gospel, a word in the wild.

possessions.name: Aeolian Scream
possessions.description: Scarce A curse from the lips of a forgotten god. Music like cold-forged iron spearheads. The Bazaar will pay handsomely!
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wild Words items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Correspondence Plaque
possessions.description: Scarce A lead plaque incised, carefully, with Correspondence sigils. Not too many. Apparently even lead can burn.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wild Words items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Maniac's Prayer
possessions.description: Scarce Perhaps you can distinguish ghastly truths in here? Possibly it just rhymes funny.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wild Words items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Primordial Shriek
possessions.description: Commonplace A scream sealed into a stone jar with black beeswax. Something left over from the dawn of time.

possessions.name: Bottle of Black Wings Absinthe
possessions.nameAndLevel: 6 x Bottle of Black Wings Absinthe
possessions.description: Another of Mr Wines' creations. Is the liquid in the bottle actually moving independently? It's hard to be sure.

possessions.name: Bottle of Broken Giant 1844
possessions.description: Commonplace Rich and dark as blood. This vintage claims to predate the Fall.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wines items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Bottle of Greyfields 1868 First Sporing
possessions.nameAndLevel: 4 x Bottle of Greyfields 1868 First Sporing
possessions.description: A vintage often reserved for the intimates and proteges of Mr Wines.

possessions.name: Bottle of Greyfields 1882
possessions.description: Commonplace One of the better years. Tangy, with an agaric aftertaste.

possessions.name: Bottle of Morelways 1872
possessions.description: Scarce "Come quickly, I am drinking the fallen stars!"
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wines items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Bottle of Strangling Willow Absinthe
possessions.nameAndLevel: 98 x Bottle of Strangling Willow Absinthe
possessions.description: Commonplace Get it off! GET IT OFF!
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wines items in your inventory.

possessions.name: Vial of Tears of the Bazaar
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Vial of Tears of the Bazaar
possessions.description: Coveted A tiny flask of utter sorrow. The touch of the corked flask provokes weeping. Consume it, and be fatally consumed by melancholy.
possessions.availableAt: Occasionally, this can be found in the Forgotten Quarter; you may also exchange Fourth City Airag for it on a certain Opportunity card...

possessions.name: Bottle of Greyfields 1879
possessions.description: Commonplace The mushroom wines of the Neath are an acquired taste.

possessions.name: Cellar of Wine
possessions.description: Scarce What civilised person could be without one? This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as joining the Young Stags' Club.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Wines items in your inventory, or earn them on the fourth coil of the Labyrinth of Tigers.

possessions.name: Bottle of Fourth City Airag: Year of the Tortoise
possessions.nameAndLevel: 5 x Bottle of Fourth City Airag: Year of the Tortoise
possessions.description: Coveted At last you find an obliging Khaganian who can translate the faded label: 'For the Khan of Dreams'. The liquid inside is milky and sour.
possessions.availableAt: Trade for it in the card A Presumptuous Opportunity, in London. Expeditions in the Forgotten Quarter occasionally uncover them.

possessions.name: Ambiguous Eolith
possessions.description: Rare A thinking creature made this, or the sea made this; or both.

possessions.name: Carved Ball of Stygian Ivory
possessions.nameAndLevel: 7 x Carved Ball of Stygian Ivory
possessions.description: Coveted An intricately carved ball of black zee-monster bones. Monsters chase zailors chase monsters around its circumference.

possessions.name: Deep-zee Catch
possessions.description: Commonplace It might be some sort of fish, but best not to look too closely.

possessions.name: Live Specimen
possessions.description: Rare Something wriggling and peculiar. It's safely confined, probably.

possessions.name: Moon-Pearl
possessions.description: Down here, these are the only way to know what phase the moon is in.

possessions.name: Antique Ring Worth Killing For
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x Antique Ring Worth Killing For
possessions.description: You were left this in a will by an elderly matron. Her family covet it.

possessions.name: Brass Ring
possessions.description: Scarce A mark of the wearer's allegiance to Hell. A foolish affectation among young blades in Fallen London. Although it does suggest a certain devil-may-care glamour.
possessions.availableAt: Create this by using other Infernal items in your inventory, or by using a Tale of Terror!! to pursue the story of the Fidgeting Writer.

possessions.name: Bully Belvedere
possessions.description: A classic from a vanished Ladybones tobacconist who pined for the zee. Sweet to the smoker, noxious to anyone else. It burns so fiercely, flammable headwear is disadvised.

possessions.name: Dilmun Club Lapel Badge
possessions.description: Opens a surprising number of doors, to those few in the know.

possessions.name: Justificande Cufflinks
possessions.description: They catch the light most dashingly during handshakes, when promises are sealed and broken, and forgiveness is in short supply.

possessions.name: Light-Drinking Cravat
possessions.description: Thirsty bombazine embroidered with spider silk, for a gleam of silver against the darkest black.

possessions.name: Mourning Locket
possessions.description: They'll never look in here. Large enough to hold a ratwork compass, a modest diamond, and a bullet.

possessions.name: Pair of Neathglass Goggles
possessions.description: Neathglass is found on black pebble beaches by candle-light. These retain a little of that light.

possessions.name: Venom-Ruby Lure
possessions.description: A pendant that draws the attention of the room, inexorably, towards your neck.

possessions.name: Viscountess' Bejewelled Collar
possessions.description: The bedraggled, bejewelled collar of a noble cat. One of a pair, it symbolises steadfastness, union and the endurance of the feline spirit.

possessions.name: Viscount's Bejewelled Collar
possessions.description: The bedraggled, bejewelled collar of a noble cat. Its great size and exact match of the Viscountess' own collar prove its provenance.

possessions.name: Mr Sacks' Surplus Sack
possessions.description: Did it really belong to the Crimson Beast of Winter, or is it just cheap hessian? Perhaps it doesn't matter; it's plenty large enough for the essentials.

possessions.name: Shabti-Box
possessions.description: "I fall at the feet of the king, my lord, my god, my Sun, seven times and seven times. I looked this way, and I looked that way, and there was no light. Then I looked towards the king, my lord, and there was light."

possessions.name: A Ragtag Gang of Outlaws
possessions.nameAndLevel: 1 x A Ragtag Gang of Outlaws
possessions.description: Assign them duties belowdecks. Ignore the sigils that glow within the darkness of their oilskins. Maintain the polite fiction that they are people.

possessions.name: Most Presentable Company
possessions.description: Spick, span, ship-shape and starched. The kind of crew one can bring home to mother.

possessions.name: Rabble of Revellers
possessions.description: Are they good at their jobs? No, not really. But they are having a marvellous time.

possessions.name: Shipful of Schemers
possessions.description: They attend to their duties with the fastidiousness only possessed by those with much to hide.

possessions.name: Zeazoned Zailors
possessions.description: "A good zailor should have exactly one scar." — M. Demeaux, Advice for Captains

possessions.name: Bessemer Steel Ingot
possessions.description: Neathy Iron, treated by Surface methods.
possessions.availableAt: This can be obtained at the Bone Market.

possessions.name: Nevercold Brass Sliver
possessions.description: Commonplace Hell's chief export. You thought that was souls? No, they don't export souls.

possessions.name: Nightsoil of the Bazaar
possessions.description: Collected from the Side-Streets. Eyewateringly pungent.
possessions.availableAt: This can be obtained at the Bone Market.

possessions.name: Perfumed Gunpowder
possessions.description: Smells like roses, leaves pink scorch marks.
possessions.availableAt: This can be achieved in small quantities by studying an Ambiguous Eolith at your Laboratory, or in larger quantities using your Lab for manufacture, once you have the formula.

possessions.name: Railway Steel
possessions.description: Tempered and shaped to carry trains of the correct gauge. Strictly incompatible with anything the devils use on the Moloch line.
possessions.availableAt: This can be created in your Laboratory, as long as you have learned the formula and have suitable ingredients.

possessions.name: Strong-Backed Labour
possessions.description: Commonplace All the sweat and elbow-grease needed to get the job done. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as a Clay Sedan Chair.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets or acquire it at Wilmot's End or in the Clay Quarters.

possessions.name: Whirring Contraption
possessions.description: Commonplace A steaming contrivance of brass and steel. Used in creating Wonders of the Age. This is mainly used for creating advanced items - such as a Zubmarine.
possessions.availableAt: If you are a Person of Some Importance, you can create this in the Bazaar Sidestreets or acquire it at Wilmot's End.
